reasoning: In late May 2025 (days before the cutoff date), French President Macron publicly pushed for European partners, specifically lobbying the UK, Netherlands, and Belgium, to join France in recognizing the State of Palestine at an upcoming UN conference on the two-state solution. This conference is firmly scheduled for June 2025 (specifically June 17-20 in New York, organized alongside Saudi Arabia). Macron has explicitly stated that the conference will "give fresh impetus to the recognition." Given the strong momentum, the formal scheduling of the conference as a target action-date, and the concerted diplomatic pressure from France upon several European states, the probability of at least one UN member country officially announcing its recognition of Palestine in June 2025 is very high. While there's a small chance of delay due to US/Israeli pressure or diplomatic bottlenecks, the broad coalition and public commitments make it highly probable.
